name: Segmented Bucket-Hat Wader (Segmentus caputbalneus)

parts.shape: stacked orange circular blobs forming a totem-pole column
parts.placement: vertical torso, rising from the blue water
top of the topmost orange segment
on the uppermost segment, beneath the hat brim
n/a - implied to hide arms within segments
across the bottom of the scene, submerging the lower body

distinctive_features: body built entirely from stacked round orange segments like a bead totem
wears a flat black bucket-style hat far too wide for its head
single small red eye peeking from under the brim
no visible arms or legs, stands half-submerged in wavy blue water
appears alongside a companion red-bodied creature in a green hat, suggesting these are a matched pair species
vibe: a stoic little water-totem taking a dip in its oversized hat
